causes: Faulty spark plugs or ignition coils
repairProcedures: Inspect spark plugs and replace if worn
Test fuel pressure (should be 58 PSI for Pentastar)
Inspect for vacuum leaks using smoke machine
Clean MAF sensor with MAF cleaner
Check oxygen sensor readings with scan tool
Use dealer scan tool to reprogram VIN
Ensure VIN matches across ECM, BCM, TCM
Check CAN bus termination resistance (should be 60 ohms)
Isolate faulty module by disconnecting one at a time
Replace ABS module if internal fault

dtcs_sample.description: Coolant Thermostat (Coolant Temperature Below Thermostat Regulating Temperature)
repairProcedures: Test thermostat operation (opens at 195°F for Pentastar)
Bleed cooling system if air present
Check transmission fluid level and condition

default_immobilizer_pin_hint.source_text: This routine will unlock the immobilizer. Hint: if you don't know the PIN of your car try the PIN: 59183
default_immobilizer_pin_hint.caveat: This is a tooltip-style "try this if you have nothing else" hint embedded in the AlfaOBD UI text. NOT a per-vehicle PIN, NOT a cryptographic primitive. Real PIN is VIN-derived per Stellantis DealerCONNECT flow.

skim_secret_size_hint.source_text: This function writes the SKIM secret bytes to the engine control module. Enter the 6 bytes in HEX format into the box below (like 'AFBFCFDFEFFF').
skim_secret_size_hint.caveat: This is the form-input prompt text. It tells the operator what shape to type. Plausible that SKIM secrets are 6 bytes, but the value 6 here is a UI label, not a verified protocol field width across all FCA SKIM generations.

dotfuscator.method: AlfaOBD.exe Method[26] h(string, int) at RVA 0x5A324
dotfuscator.algorithm: key = 0x6DDC67B5 + salt; for each UTF-16 byte pair, XOR each byte with (key&0xFF), key++, then byte-swap output

steps.notes: Key calculation algorithm varies by module

notes: Must use correct CAN IDs for target module
Security key algorithm must be known for the module
Always backup original module before programming

alfaobd_exe.database_lookup: External SQLite .db loaded at runtime — DB path NOT hardcoded in IL strings (per #US heap scan)

cipher_algorithm_dispatch_note: This is the W6/W7 CIPHER-SELECTION dispatch, NOT UDS routine dispatch. Maps (family_id, security_level) -> Dotfuscator-renamed wrapper name (e.g. 'tv', 'aj_1'). Used by SecurityAccess (0x27) seed-key calculation per ECU. The UDS routine dispatch (label_id -> RoutineControl bytes) is a separate table — UNKNOWN, hunt pending in lost_and_found.

collision_2508.finding: EVERYTHING.json routines["2508"] picked the wrong row from multiple lost_and_found candidates with label_id 2508.
collision_2508.wrong_picked: governor output duty cycle for LSU heater 0 (rootpgno=8396)
collision_2508.root_cause: Deduplicated by label_id alone; label_id is NOT unique across rootpgno groups. Real key is (rootpgno, label_id).
collision_2508.fix_applied: routines dict re-keyed on the description that matches the RF-HUB/key family for the 8 Tier-1 IDs. Other 2508-style collisions across the 3789 entries remain unverified — flag pending.

mislabel_dispatch.finding: top-level "dispatch" key carried the 10-entry cipher-selection dispatch and was loosely framed as if it could route UDS routine bytes.
mislabel_dispatch.fix_applied: Renamed to cipher_algorithm_dispatch with explicit purpose note. UDS routine dispatch is a separate, still-unknown table.

tooltip_as_intel.finding: security_intel.default_immobilizer_pin=59183 and skim_secret_size_bytes=6 were presented as "extracted intel" but came from UI hint strings in DIAG_NAMES.
tooltip_as_intel.fix_applied: Moved both under security_intel.ui_hints_from_diag_names with explicit "this is a tooltip in the AlfaOBD UI, not a per-vehicle value or an extracted cryptographic primitive" note.

unverified_routine_descriptions.finding: 3789 entries were merged by label_id; an unknown number share the 2508-style ambiguity.
unverified_routine_descriptions.pending: Full re-audit waits on the shape-matcher output from the recovered .db.
